Description.Comment:
• In 2007, the overall condition of reef habitats was classified as Insuficient data.
  • The cover of hard corals is similar to historical reference levels. At current levels hard corals are likely to be supporting positive reef accretion at all reefs monitored.
  • Encouragingly, during the most recent recovery periods, rates of increase in hard coral cover have been consistent with historical observations.
  • Of the 4 reefs monitored recovery performance was below their historical reference range at 1.
  • Additionally, at 3 reefs the current observation suggests a decline in recovery performance relative to their recent trajectories.
  • The median density of juvenile corals was below historical reference levels, Detracting from the assessment of coral communities. Juvenile densities were below reference levels at 2 of the 4 reefs assessed.
  • However the current densities of Acropora juveniles are sufficient to promote recovery at all reefs.
  • The representation of macroalgae species within the benthic algal communities was similar to historical reference levels.
  • At current levels macroalgae are unlikely to be limiting coral community resilience on any reefs monitored.
  • Although the cover of hard corals is low, there is no evidence for region-wide change in the taxonomic composition of coral communities.
Description.Disclaimer: Disclaimer: This synopsis was derived from indicators estimated at 4 reef and depth combinations. In 2007, all indicators were assessed at each location.
